revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikama-jinja]][[ja:伊河麻神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊河麻神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikama Shrine | native_name = 伊河麻神社 | image = Ikama jinja in shizuoka 02.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.966538|138.389871}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ikama Shrine'''|伊河麻神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|ja|駿河国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11668489|1=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province}} ** Series ordinal: 5 ** P155: {{ill|Q3571342|ltq=Q3571342|qid=Q3571342|1=Yaizu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q134926975|ltq=Q134926975|qid=Q134926975|1=Ikeda Shrine (Shizuoka)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Suruga-ku, Shizuoka|lt=Suruga-ku|ar|سوروغا-كو|ceb|Suruga Ku|en|Suruga-ku, Shizuoka|fa|سوروگا-کو، نیگاتا|fr|Suruga-ku|id|Suruga-ku, Shizuoka|it|Suruga-ku|ja|駿河区|ko|스루가구|tr|Suruga (semt)|uk|Район Суруґа|ur|سوروگا وارڈ، شیزوکا|zh|駿河區|qid=Q1042630|1=Suruga-ku, Shizuoka}} * {{ill|Q864170|ltq=Q864170|qid=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.966538|138.389871}} == image (P18) == * Ikama jinja in shizuoka 02.jpg == OpenCorporates ID (P1320) == * jp/9080005000137 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181551 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 422-8062https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9080005000137 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikama-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市駿河区稲川1-10-15', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ikama-jinja}} [[JA:伊河麻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q111374476}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ikama Shrine |Image = [[File:Ikama jinja in shizuoka 02.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka City|Q174691|qid=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Suruga Ward|Q1042630|qid=Q1042630|1=Suruga-ku, Shizuoka}} Inagawa 1-10-15 |Coordinates = {{coord|34|57|59.7|N|138|23|23.5|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; formerly {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 4 (664, or 675) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other name = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = September 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ikama Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|57|59.7|N|138|23|23.5|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|57|59.7|N|138|23|23.5|E}}}} }} '''Ikama Shrine''' (いかまじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka City|Q174691|qid=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Suruga Ward|Q1042630|qid=Q1042630|1=Suruga-ku, Shizuoka}}. It is the Fourth Shrine (Shinomiya) among the former “Five Shrines” of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|qid=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. The Five Shrines, according to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, are {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|Q653180|qid=Q653180|lt=Fuji Sengen Shrine|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (First Shrine), {{ill|lt=Toyozumi Shrine|Q18337536|qid=Q18337536|1=Toyozumi Shrine}} (Second Shrine), {{ill|lt=Miho Shrine|Q11489249|qid=Q11489249|1=Miho Shrine (Q11489249)}} (Third Shrine), '''Ikama Shrine''' (Fourth Shrine), and {{ill|lt=Sakabin Shrine|Q119929617|qid=Q119929617|1=Sakabe Shrine}} (Fifth Shrine)“Kojima Village History,” p.178. === Enshrined Deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association, Shizuoka Branch; Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Parishioners Association, Shizuoka Branch, “Shizuoka City Shrine Directory” (1976), p.127 ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}“Abe District, Shizuoka Prefecture Gazetteer,” p.687“Ōsato Village History, Lower Volume (1),” Chapter 7, Shrine and Temple Legends) === History === === Founding === April of {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|qid=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 4 (664, or 675) === General History === The Shogun Gunshinkaichō records it as “once a venerable and imposing shrine”. Around the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era, {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|qid=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} is said to have burned it, stating that he would rebuild the structures if he won the battle. However, it was never rebuilt, and the present shrine is said to have been the inner sanctuary (okuiin) of that time. === Court Rank === The Shogun Gunshinkaichō lists it as “Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, Igama Myōjin”. === Shrine Grounds === The following shrines are enshrined within the grounds. * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} (Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama no Kami|Q2280323|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukanomitama}}) * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|qid=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} (Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}) * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Hi-ume no Mitama Shrine|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} (Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Kamado no Kami|Q249178|qid=Q249178|lt=Okutsuhiko no Kami and Okutsuhime no Kami|1=Kitchen God}}) Ikama jinja in shizuoka 01.jpg|Torii gate and shrine grounds Ikama jinja in shizuoka 03.jpg|Sacred tree ({{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}) Ikama jinja in shizuoka 04.jpg|Subsidiary shrines (Inari Shrine and Kotohira Shrine) === Festivals === ; {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} * September 15 === Other === The kanji and names differ slightly depending on the source. * “Ikawa (Ka) 麻 Shrine, Udo District, Suruga Province” / “Ikama Shrine” — {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} * “Senior Fourth Rank Ikama Myōjin” — Shogun Gunshinkaichō * “Ikama Daimyōjin” — Shin Fudoki === Access === 500 m south of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|qid=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nankan Line|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefectural Route 407 Shizuoka–Kusanagi–Shimizu Line|Q11663505|qid=Q11663505|lt=Prefectural Route 407|1=Shizuoka Prefectural Road Route 407}}). * Bus stop: “Ikama Shrine” === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406089 Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association HP – Ikama Shrine] * “Abe District, Shizuoka Prefecture Gazetteer” (Abe District Times Company, 1914) * “Ōsato Village History, Lower Volume (1),” Chapter 7, Shrine and Temple Legends * “Kojima Village History” (1961) * “[https://multi.tosyokan.pref.shizuoka.jp/digital-library/detail?tilcod=0000000027-SZ01005044 Abe District Town and Village Gazetteers 29: Ōsato Village History, Lower Volume]” (Shizuoka Prefectural Library Digital Library, images 5–8) * Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association, Shizuoka Branch; Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Parishioners Association, Shizuoka Branch, “Shizuoka City Shrine Directory” (1976) === Related Items === {{commonscat|Ikama-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|Q11668489|qid=Q11668489|1=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province}} === External Links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406089 Ikama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefecture Shrine Association {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikama Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]]
